reviews.author: Joshua Hahn
reviews.summary:
reviewer suggested moving a comment from vmscan.c to memory-tiers.c for clarity on the behavior of @demote_folios

reviewer noted that the patch series appears mostly correct but did not provide any specific technical feedback or concerns
reviews.raw_body:
Hi Alexandre,

I hope you are doing well! Thank you for the patch.

Makes sense to move the migration functions together. Just one small
nit, I think the following comment is pretty helpful in understanding
that folios that aren't demoted still remain in @demote_folios. Should
we also move this comment to memory-tiers.c?

[...snip...]

---

[...snip...]

Anyways, the rest looks good to me. Have a great day!
Joshua

reviews.author: Joshua Hahn
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that node_get_allowed_targets() is unclear and suggested renaming it to node_get_allowed_demotion_targets, but ultimately approved the patch (LGTM) due to limited usage.
reviews.raw_body:
Agreed, node_get_allowed_targets is pretty vague ; -)

I do think that node_get_allowed_demotion_targets could be considered
a bit too long, but I don't think it's called in too many places where
there would be more than just this function call in the line so LGTM!

Please feel free to add my review tag, have a great day Alexandre!

Reviewed-by: Joshua Hahn <joshua.hahnjy@gmail.com>

reviews.author: Andrew Morton
reviews.summary: reviewer requested separation of cc:stable fixes from cleanups and preparation against current mainline, also asked for detailed changelogs describing the userspace-visible runtime effects of backportable bug fixes
reviews.raw_body:
Please let's have the cc:stable fixes separated out from the cleanups,
and prepared against current -linus mainline.

Also, when proposing backportable fixes please ensure that the
changelogs carefully describe the userspace-visible runtime effects of
the bug.

Thanks.

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner
reviews.summary: Johannes Weiner noted that the original patch's Fixes: comment was intended to disable kswapd for THP allocations while still allowing direct reclaim, and suggested a better comment would be /* GFP_TRANSHUGE has its own reclaim policy */. He also pointed out that after the fix, direct reclaim isn't allowed either, making the request unnecessarily restrictive.
reviews.raw_body:
I don't think this is right.

The Fixes: did it this way to disable kswapd for THP allocations,
while still allowing the customary direct reclaim. Maybe a better
comment would have been: /* GFP_TRANSHUGE has its own reclaim policy */

After your fix, direct reclaim isn't allowed either, which makes the
request unnecessarily wimpy.

The Closes: refers to reclaim that should be avoided during demotion.
But if this path is taken during demotion it will already not recurse
into direct reclaim due to PF_MEMALLOC.

So I don't see a bug in the existing code. But maybe the comment could
be clearer.

2026-03-11.patch_summary: This patch series cleans up and fixes demotion-related functions in the Linux kernel. It moves related functions to a new file, renames some functions for clarity, and corrects issues with GFP flags used during demotion. The changes aim to improve the memory management system by making it more explicit and efficient.

reviews.author: Byungchul Park
reviews.summary: Reviewer suggested renaming the function node_get_allowed_targets() to node_get_demotion_targets(), indicating an improvement over previous versions
reviews.raw_body:
Look better than before to me.

What about just node_get_demotion_targets()?

	Byungchul

reviews.author: Donet Tom
reviews.summary:
reviewer noted that the patch does not address the issue of vswap_free() acquiring the per-vswap spinlock while holding the folio lock, creating a potential deadlock

Reviewer questioned the necessity of the 'mt_' prefix in demote_folios() function name, suggesting it may not be needed since the function already implies demotion to a lower tier
reviews.raw_body:
Hi Alexander

On 3/11/26 4:32 PM, Alexandre Ghiti wrote:

---

Demotion will happen only when different memory tiers are present, 
right? Since demote_folios() already implies that the folios are being 
demoted to a lower tier, is the mt_ prefix needed in the function name? 
I\u2019m fine with keeping it as is, but I just wanted to clarify.

Otherwise it LGTM

Reviewed by: Donet Tom <donettom@linux.ibm.com>

reviews.author: Donet Tom
reviews.summary: Reviewer Donet Tom noted that the patch series appears to be correct and free of issues, but did not provide any specific technical feedback or suggestions for improvement.
reviews.raw_body:
This looks LGTM

Reviewed by: Donet Tom <donettom@linux.ibm.com>

reviews.author: David (Arm)
reviews.summary:
Reviewer David requested two-tab indentation on specific lines to fit within a single line, citing consistency with the existing function alloc_demote_folio() as an example.

reviewer questioned the relevance of the patch series, asking against which branch it was tested and noting that the function in question has changed since the initial issue was reported
reviews.raw_body:
use two-tab indentation on second parameter line please. So this fits
into a single line. Same for the other functions.

Just like alloc_demote_folio() that you are moving already did.

[...]

---

I think this function changed in the meantime in mm/mm-unstable. Against
which branch is this patch?

-- 
Cheers,

David

reviews.author: David (Arm)
reviews.summary: Reviewer David suggested renaming node_get_allowed_targets() to a more descriptive function name, specifically mt_get_node_demotion_targets(), and gave it a positive review.
reviews.raw_body:
+1

Maybe throw in the mt_ prefix and call it

mt_get_node_demotion_targets()

-- 
Cheers,

David

reviews.author: David (Arm)
reviews.summary: Reviewer David requested that critical fixes be sent as separate patches and applied first, rather than being included in a larger series of demotion cleanup changes.
reviews.raw_body:
Also, please move fixes to the very beginning of the patch set, or
better, send them independently.

-- 
Cheers,

David

reviews.author: Gregory Price
reviews.summary: reviewer noted that the GFP flags are being masked and added back in multiple layers during demotion, making it difficult to reason about their actual values
reviews.raw_body:
The bigger issue how many times we see this particular flag getting
masked and apparently added back in at multiple layers. We saw two
or three paths (some unreachable) that can twiddle RECLAIM flags in
the stack for demotion (which is in reclaim already, so do the flags
matter?).

It makes it difficult to reason about what the GFP flags actually
are at any given point.

But yeah I wasn't sure to make of this code, it could be as you
suggested just a bad comment.

~Gregory

2026-03-12.patch_summary: This patch series cleans up and fixes demotion-related functions in the Linux kernel. It moves related functions to a new file, renames some functions for clarity, and corrects issues with GFP flags used during demotion. The changes aim to improve the memory management system by making it more explicit and efficient.

reviews.author: Alexandre Ghiti (author)
reviews.summary:
Author acknowledged that the swapoff path needs to drop the per-vswap spinlock before calling try_to_unmap(), agreed to restructure in v2.

Author acknowledged Donet Tom's concern that the term 'demote' implies memory tiers and agreed to keep the mt_ prefix for consistency

Author acknowledged that the swapoff path needs to drop the per-vswap spinlock before calling try_to_unmap(), agreed to restructure in v2.
reviews.raw_body:
Hi Tom,

On 3/12/26 09:44, Donet Tom wrote:

---

You're right, demote implies some memory tiers. But I like the mt_ 
prefix, some functions in memory-tiers.c already have this prefix so it 
adds consistency: so since you don't mind, I'll keep it :)

---

Thanks for your time!

Alex
